title: Stenogyne leptophylla
intro: Stenogyne leptophylla, also known as Hawaiian mountain mint, is a species of flowering plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae) native to Hawaii. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, and its extracts have been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which may contribute to its potential health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, and supporting immune function. The bioactive constituents of Stenogyne leptophylla include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its pharmacological activities. In traditional Hawaiian medicine, Stenogyne leptophylla is typically prepared as a tea or a tincture, and it is also used in modern herbal medicine as a topical application or an oral supplement to support overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Stenogyne leptophylla belongs to the family Lamiaceae. This family, commonly known as the mint family, is a large and diverse group of flowering plants.
